enrichment.short_description: GatherUp is a review generation, monitoring, and reputation management platform for businesses and agencies.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: GatherUp review management software: collect reviews, monitor 100+ sites, and reply with AI. Plans start at $99/month.

features.title: Review Generation
features.description: Collects reviews through automated email and SMS requests, a website chat widget, and integrations with POS/CRM systems.

features.title: Centralized Review Monitoring
features.description: Tracks reviews across more than 100 review sites, including Google, Facebook, TripAdvisor, Trustpilot, and BBB, from one dashboard.

features.title: AI-Generated Replies
features.description: Suggests on-brand responses to customer reviews to speed up reply workflows.

features.title: Fake Review Detection
features.description: Flags suspicious reviews and files disputes automatically as part of the platform's Defend tools.

features.title: Listings Hub
features.description: An add-on that syncs business information across 125+ directories for local SEO.

features.title: Agency White-Labeling
features.description: The Agency plan lets agencies resell the platform under their own brand.

features.title: Reporting & Sentiment Analysis
features.description: Performance dashboards and sentiment tagging on incoming reviews.

pros: Monitors reviews across 100+ sites from a single dashboard
AI-generated reply suggestions speed up review responses
Built-in fake review detection with automatic dispute filing
Free trial requires no credit card
Agency plan supports white-label reselling
cons: Review Defense add-on and Agency plan pricing require contacting sales for a quote
Multi-location pricing is charged per location ($60/month each), which can add up for larger chains
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published on the site

faqs.question: Does GatherUp offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, GatherUp offers a free trial that does not require a credit card.

faqs.question: How much does GatherUp cost?
faqs.answer: The Small Business plan starts at $99/month for one location; Multi-Location pricing is $60/month per location for 2-10 locations, with volume pricing beyond that. Agency pricing requires a custom quote.

faqs.question: Which review sites does GatherUp monitor?
faqs.answer: GatherUp monitors more than 100 review sites, including Google, Facebook, TripAdvisor, Trustpilot, and BBB.

faqs.question: Does GatherUp use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, GatherUp provides AI-generated reply suggestions and AI-generated insights to help teams respond to and act on reviews faster.

faqs.question: Can agencies white-label GatherUp?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Agency plan provides white-label reputation management software that agencies can resell to clients under their own brand.

use_cases.title: Local business review generation
use_cases.description: A single-location business uses email and SMS requests to collect more Google and Facebook reviews after each customer interaction.

use_cases.title: Multi-location review monitoring
use_cases.description: A multi-location brand consolidates review monitoring and reporting for all locations into one dashboard.

use_cases.title: Agency reputation management reselling
use_cases.description: A digital agency white-labels GatherUp to offer reputation management as a service to its clients.

use_cases.title: Review fraud defense
use_cases.description: A business facing suspicious or fake reviews uses GatherUp's Defend features to detect and dispute them automatically.

features: Same features as Small Business plan
White-label reputation management software for clients

long_form_blocks.heading: What is GatherUp?
paragraphs: GatherUp is a reputation management platform that helps businesses and agencies collect, monitor, and respond to online reviews. It combines review generation via email and SMS, centralized monitoring across more than 100 review sites, and AI-generated reply suggestions in one dashboard.

The platform also includes fake review detection and dispute filing tools, listings syndication through its Listings Hub add-on, and a white-label Agency plan that lets marketing agencies resell reputation management services under their own brand.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who GatherUp is for
paragraphs: GatherUp suits small businesses managing a single location, multi-location brands that need centralized review reporting across sites, and digital agencies that want to offer white-labeled reputation management to their clients.

enrichment.short_description: Grade.us is a white-label review management platform built for marketing agencies to manage client reviews.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Grade.us lets agencies generate, monitor, and market client reviews under white-label branding, from $99/month with a 14-day trial.

features.title: Automated Review Generation
features.description: Sends automated requests to prompt customers to leave reviews.

features.title: Multi-Platform Review Monitoring
features.description: Aggregates reviews from Google, Facebook, Yelp, and industry-specific sites into one dashboard.

features.title: Review Marketing Widgets
features.description: Tools to display and leverage collected reviews on client websites.

features.title: White-Label Branding
features.description: Agencies can rebrand the entire dashboard with their own logo and domain.

features.title: Multi-Location Listings Management
features.description: Organizes and manages multiple business locations from one account.

features.title: Reporting & Analytics
features.description: Tracks review performance across multiple brands and locations for client reporting.

pros: Purpose-built for agencies with white-label branding on the entire dashboard
14-day free trial with no credit card required
Reseller-friendly plan structure aimed at agency profit margins
Aggregates reviews from major platforms plus industry-specific sites (legal, healthcare, real estate, automotive, hospitality, restaurants)
cons: No AI features are mentioned on the site
Agency-tier pricing requires a custom quote
Multi-location pricing is charged per location ($60/month each)
Premium white-label add-on costs $440/year unless on the highest seat tier

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Grade.us offers a free 14-day trial with access to 3 seats and most platform features, no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Grade.us cost?
faqs.answer: The Small Business plan is $99/month for one location, Multi Location pricing is $60/month per location for 2-10 locations, and Agency pricing requires a custom quote.

faqs.question: Can agencies white-label Grade.us?
faqs.answer: Yes, all plans include basic white-label branding, and a Premium White Label add-on ($440/year) adds a custom domain and SSL.

faqs.question: Who owns Grade.us?
faqs.answer: Grade.us is a product of Traject.

faqs.question: Which review sites does Grade.us monitor?
faqs.answer: It monitors major platforms like Google, Facebook, and Yelp, plus industry-specific sites for sectors such as legal, healthcare, real estate, automotive, and hospitality.

use_cases.title: Agency review management reselling
use_cases.description: An agency manages review generation and monitoring for many clients from one white-labeled dashboard.

use_cases.title: Multi-location reputation reporting
use_cases.description: A multi-location brand consolidates review performance reporting across all its locations.

use_cases.title: Industry-specific review monitoring
use_cases.description: A legal or healthcare practice monitors reviews on industry-specific platforms alongside Google and Facebook.
use_cases.audience: Legal, healthcare, and real estate practices

features: White-label reputation management software for clients

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Grade.us?
paragraphs: Grade.us is a review management platform built specifically for marketing agencies. It automates review generation, aggregates monitoring from major review sites like Google, Facebook, and Yelp plus industry-specific platforms, and provides review marketing tools, all wrapped in a white-label dashboard.

The platform, owned by Traject, lets agencies apply their own branding across the entire client-facing dashboard, with an optional Premium White Label add-on for a fully custom domain and SSL certificate.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Grade.us is for
paragraphs: Grade.us is designed for marketing and SEO agencies that manage review generation and reputation reporting for multiple clients, as well as multi-location businesses that need centralized review monitoring.

enrichment.short_description: NiceJob automates review requests, referrals, and social proof marketing for local service businesses.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: NiceJob helps local businesses get more Google reviews and referrals with AI-powered automation. Plans start at $75/month.

features.title: Automated Review Requests
features.description: Sends review invitations via email and SMS after customer interactions.

features.title: AI-Powered Review Replies
features.description: Automatically generates responses to customer reviews.

features.title: Referral Automation
features.description: Invites past customers to refer new business (available on the Pro plan).

features.title: Social Proof Widgets
features.description: Displays reviews and customer activity on the business website.

features.title: NPS Surveys
features.description: Collects customer satisfaction feedback.

features.title: Website Builder (Sites)
features.description: Builds and hosts a conversion-optimized business website as an add-on service.

features.title: Booking Reminders (Repeats)
features.description: Automated reminders to encourage repeat bookings.

pros: AI-generated review reply automation
14-day free trial, no credit card required
Integrates with more than 1,000 business apps
Backed by Paystone Inc., an established business software company
No long-term contracts required
cons: Referral automation and booking reminders are only available on the higher-priced Pro plan ($125/month)
Website builder (Sites) carries an extra $199 setup fee
Enterprise/franchise pricing is not public and requires custom quoting

faqs.question: Does NiceJob offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does NiceJob cost?
faqs.answer: The Reviews plan starts at $75/month; the Pro plan, which adds referrals and booking reminders, is $125/month.

faqs.question: Does NiceJob use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes AI-powered review reply generation to help businesses respond to customer reviews faster.

faqs.question: Where is NiceJob based?
faqs.answer: NiceJob is headquartered in London, Ontario, Canada, and is part of Paystone Inc.

faqs.question: Can NiceJob build a website?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Sites product builds a custom business website, available standalone at $99/month plus a $199 setup fee, or bundled with the Grow plan for $174/month plus setup.

use_cases.title: Home services review generation
use_cases.description: An HVAC or plumbing company automates review requests after each job to build a stronger online reputation.

use_cases.title: Referral-driven growth
use_cases.description: A local business on the Pro plan automates customer referral invitations alongside review requests.
use_cases.audience: Local service businesses seeking referral growth

use_cases.title: Website plus reputation bundle
use_cases.description: A business without an existing site uses the Grow + Sites bundle to get both a new website and review automation.
use_cases.audience: Businesses needing a new website and reputation tools

long_form_blocks.heading: What is NiceJob?
paragraphs: NiceJob is a reputation marketing platform for local service businesses that automates review requests, referral invitations, and social proof. It uses AI to help generate review replies and offers NPS surveys, booking reminders, and a website builder as part of its product suite.

Owned by Paystone Inc. and headquartered in London, Ontario, Canada, NiceJob is built for home services, professional services, health and wellness, hospitality, and multi-location franchise businesses.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who NiceJob is for
paragraphs: NiceJob is aimed at local service businesses such as HVAC, plumbing, and landscaping companies, as well as professional and hospitality businesses that want to automate review generation, referrals, and customer communication.

enrichment.short_description: Broadly is an AI platform that automates lead capture, reputation management, and marketing for local service businesses.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Broadly bundles an AI receptionist, review management, and social posting for local service businesses at $799/month.

features.title: AI Receptionist
features.description: Captures leads and books appointments automatically via phone, chat, and text.

features.title: AI Reputation Specialist
features.description: Automates review response and review request generation.

features.title: AI Social Media Manager
features.description: Drafts and schedules social media content.

features.title: AI Sales Assistant
features.description: Transcribes meetings and analyzes sales calls.

features.title: Local Listings Management
features.description: Syncs business information across 70+ directories.

features.title: Unified Messaging Inbox
features.description: Consolidates communications from multiple channels into one inbox.

features.title: Professional Website
features.description: A business website is built and launched as part of the package.

pros: Bundles an AI receptionist, reputation, social, and sales tools into a single package
No long-term contract required
Integrates with ServiceTitan, Jobber, Clio, and QuickBooks
Includes a professional website built within 7 days
No setup fees or per-user charges according to the site
cons: Single flat-rate package ($799/month) with no lower-cost tier for smaller budgets
Full pricing details and discounts require speaking with a sales representative
Founding year and company size are not published on the site

faqs.question: How much does Broadly cost?
faqs.answer: Broadly's AI Workforce Package is $799/month, billed month-to-month with no long-term contract.

faqs.question: What does the AI Receptionist do?
faqs.answer: It captures leads and books appointments automatically through phone calls, text messages, and web chat.

faqs.question: Does Broadly manage online reviews?
faqs.answer: Yes, its AI Reputation Specialist automates review responses and sends review requests to customers.

faqs.question: Which business tools does Broadly integrate with?
faqs.answer: Broadly integrates with ServiceTitan, Jobber, Clio, and Intuit QuickBooks, among others.

faqs.question: Where is Broadly headquartered?
faqs.answer: Broadly is headquartered in Boca Raton, Florida.

use_cases.title: Missed-call lead recovery
use_cases.description: A home services contractor uses the AI Receptionist to capture and follow up with leads from missed calls.

use_cases.title: Automated review generation and response
use_cases.description: A dental or law practice automates review requests and AI-generated responses to build its online reputation.

use_cases.title: All-in-one local marketing
use_cases.description: A local business consolidates its website, listings, social posting, and reputation management under one AI-driven package.
use_cases.audience: Local service businesses wanting a single vendor for marketing

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Broadly?
paragraphs: Broadly is an AI-powered platform for local service businesses that combines an AI receptionist, review response automation, social media posting, and sales call analysis into a single monthly package. It is headquartered in Boca Raton, Florida.

The platform's AI Workforce Package costs $799/month and includes a built website, listings syndication to more than 70 directories, and integrations with field service and practice management tools like ServiceTitan, Jobber, Clio, and QuickBooks.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Broadly is for
paragraphs: Broadly targets local service businesses such as HVAC companies, plumbers, electricians, auto repair shops, dental practices, and law firms that want to consolidate lead capture, reputation management, and marketing into one AI-driven package.

enrichment.short_description: SOCi is an AI marketing platform that deploys autonomous agents to manage local SEO, social, and reputation for multi-location brands.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SOCi uses autonomous 'Genius Agents' to automate local SEO, social media, and reputation management for multi-location brands.

features.title: Genius Search Agent
features.description: Automates local SEO optimizations based on monthly data analysis.

features.title: Genius Social Agent
features.description: Automatically builds social content calendars and manages engagement.

features.title: Genius Reputation Agent
features.description: Generates brand-appropriate responses to customer reviews.

features.title: Local Pages
features.description: SEO-optimized landing pages for individual business locations.

features.title: Boost & Ads
features.description: Auto-generates hyper-localized ad campaigns.

features.title: Chat
features.description: 24/7 localized chatbots across multiple channels.

features.title: Locator
features.description: Searchable location finder pages for multi-location brands.

pros: Autonomous 'Genius Agents' automate SEO, social, and reputation tasks rather than just assisting with them
Reports having deployed 200,000+ local agents across 500+ enterprise brands
Mobile app ('SOCi Go!') available for iOS and Android
Covers search, social, reputation, ads, and chat in a single platform
cons: No public pricing; requires requesting a demo
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published on the site
Positioned for enterprise multi-location brands, which may not suit small single-location businesses

faqs.question: What is SOCi?
faqs.answer: SOCi is an AI-powered marketing platform for multi-location brands that uses autonomous 'Genius Agents' to automate local SEO, social media, reputation management, and paid ads.

faqs.question: Does SOCi offer public pricing?
faqs.answer: No, SOCi's website does not list public pricing; businesses must request a demo.

faqs.question: What are SOCi's 'Genius Agents'?
faqs.answer: They are AI agents branded for specific functions: Genius Search Agent for local SEO, Genius Social Agent for social content, and Genius Reputation Agent for review responses.

faqs.question: Is there a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, SOCi offers a mobile app called 'SOCi Go!' for iOS and Android.

faqs.question: Who uses SOCi?
faqs.answer: SOCi is built for enterprise brands and franchises managing multiple locations across industries like restaurants, retail, financial services, healthcare, and property management.

use_cases.title: Multi-location local SEO automation
use_cases.description: A multi-location retail or restaurant brand uses the Genius Search Agent to automate local SEO optimizations across all locations.

use_cases.title: Automated social content at scale
use_cases.description: A franchise brand uses the Genius Social Agent to generate and publish localized social content across hundreds of location pages.

use_cases.title: Automated review response
use_cases.description: An enterprise brand uses the Genius Reputation Agent to generate on-brand responses to customer reviews at scale.
use_cases.audience: Enterprise brands managing high review volume

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SOCi?
paragraphs: SOCi is an AI-powered marketing platform built for multi-location and franchise brands. It deploys autonomous 'Genius Agents' that handle local SEO, social media publishing, and review response without requiring manual execution for every location.

The platform also includes local landing pages, hyper-localized ad generation, 24/7 chat, and location-finder pages. SOCi reports deploying more than 200,000 local agents across over 500 enterprise brands.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SOCi is for
paragraphs: SOCi is designed for enterprise brands and franchises managing multiple locations in industries such as restaurants, retail, financial services, healthcare, and property management.

enrichment.short_description: Yext is an enterprise platform for managing brand visibility, listings, and reputation across search engines and AI search.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Yext manages brand data, listings, and reviews across search engines and AI platforms for enterprise brands with many locations.

features.title: Scout
features.description: Monitors AI search visibility and tracks competitors in real time.

features.title: Knowledge Graph
features.description: Centralizes and manages brand data for search and AI experiences.

features.title: Listings
features.description: Manages location information across the web.

features.title: Reviews
features.description: Reputation management and rating optimization tools.

features.title: Pages
features.description: Search-optimized local landing pages.

features.title: Social
features.description: Local social media management.

features.title: Hearsay Social/Relate
features.description: Compliance-focused engagement tools for financial advisors.

pros: Actively monitors and manages brand visibility in AI search platforms, not just traditional search
Used by large enterprises including Samsung, FedEx, Domino's, and IHG Hotels according to the site
Direct publisher integrations with Google Business Profile, Apple Business, Facebook, OpenAI, Bing, and TripAdvisor
Dedicated mobile app (Scout by Yext) for iOS and Android
Covers 4M+ locations worldwide per company claims
cons: No public pricing; sign-up requires a demo request
Platform is enterprise-focused, which may be more complex than needed for very small businesses
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published on the page reviewed

faqs.question: What does Yext do?
faqs.answer: Yext is an enterprise platform that manages brand data, listings, reviews, and content distribution across search engines and AI platforms.

faqs.question: Does Yext monitor AI search visibility?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Scout product monitors AI search visibility and tracks competitors in real time.

faqs.question: Is Yext pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, Yext does not publish pricing; businesses must request a demo.

faqs.question: Which platforms does Yext integrate with?
faqs.answer: Yext has direct integrations with Google Business Profile, Apple Business, Facebook, OpenAI, Bing, and TripAdvisor.

faqs.question: Is there a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Scout by Yext is available for iOS and Android.

use_cases.title: Multi-location brand data management
use_cases.description: An enterprise brand with thousands of locations uses Yext's Knowledge Graph to keep location data consistent across the web.

use_cases.title: AI search visibility monitoring
use_cases.description: A brand uses Scout to track how it appears in AI-driven search results and monitor competitors.
use_cases.audience: Marketing teams tracking AI search presence

use_cases.title: Financial advisor compliance engagement
use_cases.description: A financial services firm uses Hearsay Social/Relate to manage compliant social engagement for individual advisors.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Yext?
paragraphs: Yext is an enterprise platform that helps brands manage their data, listings, reviews, and content across traditional search engines and AI-driven search platforms. Its Knowledge Graph centralizes brand data, while products like Scout monitor AI search visibility and competitors in real time.

The platform includes listings management, reputation management, local landing pages, social media management, and compliance-focused tools for financial advisors through its Hearsay Social/Relate products. Yext reports supporting brands managing more than 4 million locations worldwide.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Yext is for
paragraphs: Yext is built for large enterprise brands managing many locations, particularly in financial services, healthcare, retail, food service, and hospitality.

enrichment.short_description: Uberall is a multi-location marketing platform for managing listings, reviews, and social presence across search and AI channels.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Uberall manages listings, reviews, and local social presence for multi-location brands, founded in 2013 in Berlin.

features.title: Listings Management
features.description: Publishes and optimizes business listings across 150+ directories including Google, Apple, and voice search.

features.title: Geo Studio
features.description: AI-powered optimization tool for multi-location visibility.

features.title: Review Management
features.description: Monitors, responds to, and analyzes customer reviews across all locations.

features.title: Local Pages & Locator
features.description: SEO-optimized location pages for discovery.

features.title: Local Social
features.description: AI-generated social content publishing across Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, Google, and Apple.

features.title: UB-I
features.description: An agentic AI product described as continuously monitoring and orchestrating location performance improvements.

pros: Founded in 2013 and headquartered in Berlin, Germany, with a documented track record
Listings sync across 150+ directories including Google, Apple Maps, and voice search
Integrates with Shopify, Salesforce, Zendesk, Tableau, Google BigQuery, Google Ads, and Semrush
Includes an agentic AI product (UB-I) for autonomous location performance monitoring
cons: No public pricing; the three tiers (Show Up, Stand Out, Connect) require requesting a quote
Additional add-ons (Reviews Plus, Analytics Plus, Collaborate Plus, Geo Studio, Social Ads) are priced separately
Company size is not disclosed

faqs.question: When was Uberall founded?
faqs.answer: Uberall was founded in 2013 by Florian Hübner and David Federhen.

faqs.question: Where is Uberall headquartered?
faqs.answer: Uberall is headquartered in Berlin, Germany.

faqs.question: Is Uberall pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, Uberall's three plans (Show Up, Stand Out, Connect) require requesting pricing directly.

faqs.question: What does UB-I do?
faqs.answer: UB-I is described as an agentic AI product that continuously monitors and orchestrates improvements to location performance.

faqs.question: Which directories does Uberall publish listings to?
faqs.answer: Uberall publishes and optimizes listings across more than 150 directories, including Google, Apple, and voice search platforms.

use_cases.title: Multi-directory listings sync
use_cases.description: A retail chain publishes and keeps consistent business listings across 150+ directories from one dashboard.

use_cases.title: Cross-location review management
use_cases.description: A hospitality brand monitors and responds to reviews across all its locations in one place.

use_cases.title: Automated local social publishing
use_cases.description: A multi-location brand uses Local Social to publish AI-generated content across Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn for each location.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Uberall?
paragraphs: Uberall is a multi-location marketing platform, founded in 2013 and headquartered in Berlin, Germany, that helps brands manage their digital presence across search, maps, AI, and social channels. It publishes and optimizes listings across more than 150 directories.

The platform also includes review management, SEO-optimized local pages, AI-generated local social content, and UB-I, an agentic AI product that continuously monitors and orchestrates location performance improvements.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Uberall is for
paragraphs: Uberall serves enterprise and mid-market brands with multiple locations, particularly in retail, quick-service restaurants, automotive, and hospitality.

enrichment.short_description: Synup is an all-in-one operations platform for agencies to manage client acquisition, listings, reviews, and marketing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Synup helps agencies manage sales, listings, reviews, social, and SEO for clients with white-label tools, from $79/month.

features.title: Agency Operations
features.description: Lead generation, sales pipeline management, task tracking, and invoicing.

features.title: Listings Management
features.description: Manages client business listings across locations.

features.title: Review Management
features.description: Monitors and responds to client reviews, including AI-assisted responses.

features.title: Social Media Management
features.description: Schedules and publishes social content for clients.

features.title: White-Labeling
features.description: Customizable branding and client portals.

features.title: Sales AI, Client AI, and Marketing AI
features.description: AI tools for prospecting, client data analysis, and content/review-response generation.

pros: Transparent, published tiered pricing starting at $79/month (annual billing)
Combines agency back-office operations (sales, invoicing) with client marketing delivery in one platform
White-label client portals and embedded marketing applications for reselling
AI tools span sales prospecting, client analytics, and marketing content/review responses
Site reports 5,000+ partners using the platform
cons: Overage fees apply for extra team members ($5) and client accounts ($1.50) beyond plan limits
Add-on modules (Listings, Reviews, Social, SEO) are priced per location/connection on top of base plans
Higher tiers (Scale plan) cost $799-$999/month, a significant jump from the Startup tier

faqs.question: How much does Synup cost?
faqs.answer: The Startup plan is $79/month billed annually (or $99/month billed monthly); the Agency plan is $199/month annually ($249 monthly); and the Scale plan is $799/month annually ($999 monthly).

faqs.question: What does the Startup plan include?
faqs.answer: It supports up to 5 team members, 25 client accounts, 50 client users, and listings/reviews management for 25 locations.

faqs.question: Does Synup use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Synup includes Sales AI for prospecting, Client AI for data analysis, and Marketing AI for content and review responses.

faqs.question: Where is Synup headquartered?
faqs.answer: Synup's address is listed as 101 Avenue of the Americas, New York, NY.

faqs.question: Are there costs beyond the base plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, add-on products like Listings, Reviews, Social, and SEO are priced separately per location or connection, and overage fees apply for extra team members and client accounts.

use_cases.title: Agency client acquisition and delivery
use_cases.description: An SEO or web design agency manages its own sales pipeline while delivering listings, reviews, and SEO services to clients from one platform.

use_cases.title: White-label client portals
use_cases.description: An agency gives clients access to a branded portal showing their listings and review performance.

use_cases.title: Scaling multi-location client management
use_cases.description: A larger agency on the Scale plan manages up to 500 client locations across listings, reviews, and SEO.
use_cases.audience: Larger agencies with many client locations

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Synup?
paragraphs: Synup is an operations platform for agencies that combines sales pipeline management, invoicing, and task tracking with client-facing marketing tools like listings, reviews, social media, and SEO management.

The platform is headquartered in New York and offers white-label client portals, embedded marketing applications, and AI tools branded as Sales AI, Client AI, and Marketing AI. Pricing is tiered by team size and number of client accounts, starting with the Startup plan at $79/month billed annually.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Synup is for
paragraphs: Synup is built for agencies, including SEO agencies, web design agencies, and franchisees, that need to run their own sales operations while delivering listings, reviews, and marketing services to multiple clients.

enrichment.short_description: BrightLocal is a local SEO platform for tracking rankings, managing listings, and monitoring reviews.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: BrightLocal tracks local search rankings, manages business listings, and monitors reviews. Plans start at $31/month, plus free tools.

features.title: Local Rank Tracking
features.description: Monitors search rankings at the neighborhood level via a Local Search Grid.

features.title: Listings Management
features.description: Keeps business information consistent across directories.

features.title: Review Monitoring
features.description: Tracks and manages customer reviews across platforms.

features.title: AI Insights
features.description: Turns local SEO data into prioritized action recommendations.

features.title: Citation Builder
features.description: Manually builds and cleans up citations to improve listing consistency.

features.title: Rank Fluctuation Monitor
features.description: Alerts users to search algorithm changes affecting rankings.

features.title: API & MCP Server
features.description: Allows third-party and AI tool integration with BrightLocal data.

pros: Transparent public pricing starting at $31/month for the All-in-One Platform
Free tools available without registration
Site reports 15,000+ active customers
Offers an MCP Server for integration with AI tools, in addition to a standard API
Managed SEO services available for businesses wanting done-for-you work
cons: Managed SEO Services start at $1,299/month, a significant jump from the self-serve platform
Citation building is billed per citation ($2 each) on top of the platform subscription
Exact founding year and headquarters city are not clearly stated on the page

faqs.question: How much does BrightLocal cost?
faqs.answer: The All-in-One Platform starts at $31/month; Build Citations starts at $2 per citation; Managed SEO Services start at $1,299/month.

faqs.question: Does BrightLocal offer free tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, free tools are available with no registration required, alongside its paid platform.

faqs.question: What is AI Insights?
faqs.answer: AI Insights is a BrightLocal feature that turns local SEO data into clear, prioritized recommended actions.

faqs.question: Can I track rankings at a neighborhood level?
faqs.answer: Yes, BrightLocal's Local Search Grid tracks rankings across specific geographic points to show neighborhood-level visibility.

faqs.question: Does BrightLocal support API access?
faqs.answer: Yes, BrightLocal offers API solutions and an MCP Server for integrating its data with AI tools.

use_cases.title: Local rank tracking for agencies
use_cases.description: An SEO agency tracks neighborhood-level rankings for multiple clients using the Local Search Grid.

use_cases.title: Listings consistency management
use_cases.description: A local business keeps its name, address, and phone number consistent across directories using the listings tools.

use_cases.title: Managed local SEO
use_cases.description: A business without an in-house SEO team hires BrightLocal's Managed SEO Services for done-for-you local optimization.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is BrightLocal?
paragraphs: BrightLocal is a local search optimization platform that helps businesses and agencies track search rankings, manage business listings, and monitor online reviews. Its Local Search Grid shows neighborhood-level ranking visibility, and its AI Insights feature turns local SEO data into prioritized recommended actions.

The platform is offered as a self-serve All-in-One Platform starting at $31/month, alongside a la carte citation building and Managed SEO Services for businesses that want done-for-you work. BrightLocal also provides an API and MCP Server for integrating its data with other tools, including AI tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who BrightLocal is for
paragraphs: BrightLocal serves local businesses seeking better local search visibility and digital agencies or SEO consultants managing local SEO for multiple clients.

enrichment.short_description: Whitespark provides local SEO software and services for Google Business Profile management, rank tracking, and citations.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Whitespark offers local SEO tools and services: GBP management, rank tracking, citations, and reputation. Founded 2005 in Edmonton.

features.title: Local Platform
features.description: Manages Google Business Profiles, prevents unwanted edits, and supports bulk location edits.

features.title: Local Ranking Grids
features.description: Monitors local rankings across up to 225 geographical points.

features.title: Local Rank Tracker
features.description: Tracks local and organic search performance.

features.title: Local Citation Finder
features.description: Identifies citation opportunities.

features.title: Reputation Builder
features.description: An automated review management platform.

features.title: Free Tools
features.description: Includes a Review Checker, Google Review Link Generator, Free Local Ranking Checker, and Freshness Distance Calculator.

features.title: Done-for-You Services
features.description: SEO Services, Listings Service, GBP Reinstatement Service, and a Yext Replacement Service.

pros: Founded in 2005 in Edmonton, Alberta, Canada, with a long operating history in local SEO
Site reports 150,000+ customers, including enterprises and established brands
Offers several standalone free tools alongside paid software
Wide range of point solutions (rank tracking, citations, GBP management, reputation) available a la carte
Local Platform starts as low as $1/month per location
cons: No AI capabilities are mentioned on the site
Product line is split across many separate tools and pricing pages, which can make total cost less clear
Professional services pricing spans a wide range ($499-$1,999/month for SEO Services), requiring a custom quote for exact cost

faqs.question: When was Whitespark founded?
faqs.answer: Whitespark was founded in 2005 in Edmonton, Alberta, Canada.

faqs.question: How much does Whitespark cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing varies by product: Local Platform starts at $1/month per location, Local Ranking Grids from $10/month, Local Rank Tracker from $14-$200/month, and Local Citation Finder from $33-$149/month.

faqs.question: Does Whitespark offer free tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers free tools including a Review Checker, Google Review Link Generator, Free Local Ranking Checker, and Freshness Distance Calculator.

faqs.question: Does Whitespark manage Google Business Profiles?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Local Platform product is built to manage Google Business Profiles, prevent unwanted updates, and support bulk edits.

faqs.question: Does Whitespark offer done-for-you SEO services?
faqs.answer: Yes, its SEO Services range from $499-$1,999/month, and it also offers Listings, GBP Reinstatement, and Yext Replacement services.

use_cases.title: Local rank tracking
use_cases.description: An agency uses the Local Rank Tracker and Local Ranking Grids to monitor client rankings across up to 225 geographic points.

use_cases.title: Google Business Profile management
use_cases.description: A multi-location business uses the Local Platform to prevent unwanted GBP edits and make bulk updates across locations.

use_cases.title: Citation building and cleanup
use_cases.description: A small business uses the Local Citation Finder and Listings Service to identify and fix inconsistent citations.
use_cases.audience: Small businesses and freelancers doing local SEO

use_cases.title: Done-for-you local SEO
use_cases.description: A business without in-house SEO expertise hires Whitespark's SEO Services for full-service local optimization.

features: Monitor rankings across up to 225 geographical points
Local and organic search performance tracking

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Whitespark?
paragraphs: Whitespark is a local SEO company, founded in 2005 in Edmonton, Alberta, Canada, that offers both software tools and professional services. Its software includes Google Business Profile management (Local Platform), rank tracking (Local Rank Tracker and Local Ranking Grids), citation discovery (Local Citation Finder), and automated review management (Reputation Builder).

Alongside its paid tools, Whitespark offers several free tools, including a Review Checker and a Google Review Link Generator, plus done-for-you services such as SEO Services, a Listings Service, GBP Reinstatement, and a Yext Replacement Service.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Whitespark is for
paragraphs: Whitespark serves small businesses, agencies, freelancers, and multi-location enterprises doing local SEO, offering both self-serve software and full-service options depending on how much hands-on support they need.
